title: Timothée Chalamet Wins Golden Globe for Best Actor in Musical or Comedy
text: Timothée Chalamet claimed his first Golden Globe award at the 83rd Golden Globe Awards ceremony on January 11, 2026, winning Best Actor in a Musical or Comedy for his role in "Marty Supreme." The victory marks a significant achievement for Chalamet, who had received four prior nominations without winning—for "Call Me By Your Name," "Beautiful Boy," "Wonka," and "A Complete Unknown." During his acceptance speech, he reflected on those previous losses, stating: "Those moments didn't make this moment that much sweeter" while noting his father taught him to remain grateful. Directed by Josh Safdie, "Marty Supreme" follows a fictional ping-pong prodigy pursuing his championship dreams. The production has been a commercial success, earning $84 million globally. Chalamet conducted an extensive promotional campaign, including climbing the Las Vegas Sphere and distributing branded merchandise. Chalamet thanked Safdie, co-writer Ronnie Bronstein, and his ensemble cast including Gwyneth Paltrow, Fran Drescher, and Kevin O'Leary. He concluded his remarks by acknowledging his family and girlfriend Kylie Jenner: "To my parents and my partner, I love you." Chalamet defeated competitors including George Clooney, Leonardo DiCaprio, Ethan Hawke, and Jesse Plemons in the category.
